curriculum.subject: English Language and Literature
curriculum.overallDescription: 3 units, 4 chapters, and 2 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage, Discursive passage of 400-450 words (10 marks), Case-based factual passage with visual input of 200-250 words (10 marks), Comprehension, Interpretation, Analysis, Inference, Evaluation, Vocabulary
topics: Case-based factual passage with visual input of 200-250 words (10 marks)

units.description: Grammar, Writing Skills, Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (10 marks), Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) (5 marks), Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) (5 marks), Accuracy, Spelling, Punctuation, Creative Expression, Reasoning, Fluency, Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words)
topics: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (10 marks)

units.description: Reference to the Context, Short & Long Answer Questions
topics: Short Answer Type from Beehive (4x3=12 marks)
Short Answer Type from Moments (3x2=6 marks)
Long Answer Type from Beehive (6 marks)
Long Answer Type from Moments (6 marks)

units.description: Assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills, Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Classroom Interaction, Participation, Expression of Ideas
topics: Assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills

formative_content_refs.title: Words and Expressions – I (Workbook for Class IX)

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment
practicals.details: Regular practice and assessment of listening and speaking skills (05 marks) involving oral activities, discussions, and presentations based on the curriculum texts.

activities.title: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage
activities.details: Students engage in reading discursive passages (400-450 words) and case-based factual passages with visual input (200-250 words). Activities include answering Multiple Choice Questions, Objective Type Questions, and Very Short Answer Questions to assess comprehension, interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Grammar Accuracy Exercises
activities.details: Students practice Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, and Reported speech through Gap Filling, Editing, and Transformation exercises. Focus is on accurate use of spelling, punctuation, and grammar.

activities.title: Descriptive Paragraph Writing
activities.details: Students write descriptive paragraphs (100-120 words) describing a person, event, or situation based on visual or verbal cues.

activities.title: Story Writing and Diary Entry
activities.details: Students write a story on a given cue/title or a diary entry (100-120 words) to demonstrate creative expression.

activities.title: Reference to the Context Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry, answering Multiple Choice Questions to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Short Answer Questions Discussion
activities.details: Students answer short answer questions (40-50 words) from Beehive and Moments to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, and evaluation.

activities.title: Long Answer Type Creative Writing
activities.details: Students answer long answer questions (100-120 words) from Beehive and Moments, focusing on creativity, imagination, extrapolation beyond the text, theme interpretation, or character sketches.

activities.title: Role Play and Skit Performance
activities.details: Students participate in role plays, skits, and dramatizations based on texts from Beehive and Moments to enhance listening and speaking skills.

activities.title: Group Discussion and Peer Interaction
activities.details: Students engage in classroom interactions, group work, and discussions to express ideas, defend views, and participate actively.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit/Dramatization
projects.details: Students create and perform art-integrated projects based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization derived from the prescribed texts (Beehive/Moments) to assess listening and speaking skills.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Total length 600-700 words.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation exercises (10 out of 12 questions attempted).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words, 5 marks) and Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words, 5 marks). One out of two options.
Reference to Context: Extract from Drama/Prose (5+5 marks) and Poetry (5+5 marks). MCQs/Objective type questions.
Short Answer Questions: From Beehive (4x3=12 marks) and Moments (3x2=6 marks). Word limit 40-50 words.
Long Answer Questions: From Beehive (6 marks) and Moments (6 marks). Word limit 100-120 words.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary.
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy.
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central theme/sub-theme, understanding writers' message.
internal_assessment: Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking activities including Role Play and Group Work.
Assessment of oral communication skills based on prescribed guidelines.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning.

competency_groups.title: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage
competency_groups.description: Assessment of conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting and vocabulary skills through discursive and case-based factual passages.

competency_groups.title: Grammar and Writing Skills
competency_groups.description: Assessment of accurate use of spelling, punctuation, grammar, creative expression, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style and tone, using appropriate format and fluency.

competency_groups.title: Reference to the Context
competency_groups.description: Assessment of interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation and vocabulary through extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry.

competency_groups.title: Short & Long Answer Questions
competency_groups.description: Assessment of interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, creativity, imagination and extrapolation beyond the text across Beehive and Moments texts.

Long Answer Questions: From Beehive (6 marks) and Moments (6 marks). Word limit 100-120 words.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary.
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy.
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central theme/sub-theme, understanding writers' message.
internal_assessment: Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking activities including Role Play and Group Work.
Assessment of oral communication skills based on prescribed guidelines.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning.

activities.title: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage
activities.details: Students engage in reading discursive passages (400-450 words) and case-based factual passages with visual input (200-250 words). Activities include answering Multiple Choice Questions, Objective Type Questions, and Very Short Answer Questions to assess comprehension, interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Grammar Accuracy Exercises
activities.details: Students practice Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, and Reported speech through Gap Filling, Editing, and Transformation exercises. Focus is on accurate use of spelling, punctuation, and grammar.

activities.title: Descriptive Paragraph Writing
activities.details: Students write descriptive paragraphs (100-120 words) describing a person, event, or situation based on visual or verbal cues.

activities.title: Story Writing and Diary Entry
activities.details: Students write a story on a given cue/title or a diary entry (100-120 words) to demonstrate creative expression.

activities.title: Reference to the Context Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry, answering Multiple Choice Questions to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Short Answer Questions Discussion
activities.details: Students answer short answer questions (40-50 words) from Beehive and Moments to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, and evaluation.

activities.title: Long Answer Type Creative Writing
activities.details: Students answer long answer questions (100-120 words) from Beehive and Moments, focusing on creativity, imagination, extrapolation beyond the text, theme interpretation, or character sketches.

activities.title: Role Play and Skit Performance
activities.details: Students participate in role plays, skits, and dramatizations based on texts from Beehive and Moments to enhance listening and speaking skills.

activities.title: Group Discussion and Peer Interaction
activities.details: Students engage in classroom interactions, group work, and discussions to express ideas, defend views, and participate actively.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit/Dramatization
projects.details: Students create and perform art-integrated projects based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization derived from the prescribed texts (Beehive/Moments) to assess listening and speaking skills.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment
practicals.details: Regular practice and assessment of listening and speaking skills (05 marks) involving oral activities, discussions, and presentations based on the curriculum texts.

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Discursive and case-based factual passages (20 marks)

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Grammar (10 marks) and Writing Skills (10 marks)

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Extract analysis from Drama/Prose and Poetry; MCQs/Objective questions
Short and long answer writing; role play, skit, dramatization

curriculumSnapshot.subject: English Language and Literature
curriculumSnapshot.overallDescription: 3 units, 4 chapters, and 2 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) and Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks). Total length 600-700 words.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation exercises (10 out of 12 questions attempted).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words, 5 marks) and Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words, 5 marks). One out of two options.
Reference to Context: Extract from Drama/Prose (5+5 marks) and Poetry (5+5 marks). MCQs/Objective type questions.
Short Answer Questions: From Beehive (4x3=12 marks) and Moments (3x2=6 marks). Word limit 40-50 words.
Long Answer Questions: From Beehive (6 marks) and Moments (6 marks). Word limit 100-120 words.
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary.
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression of opinion, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy.
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central theme/sub-theme, understanding writers' message.
internal_assessment: Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used.
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking activities including Role Play and Group Work.
Assessment of oral communication skills based on prescribed guidelines.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Projects serve to diagnose mistakes and areas of non-learning.
